meta.name: CircleTel Platform Architecture Map
meta.description: Full-system architecture inventory of the CircleTel B2B/B2C ISP platform (South Africa): frontend portals, API surface, service layer, external integrations, background jobs, database schema, middleware, auth, and CI/CD.
meta.hosting: Coolify (Docker) on self-hosted VPS 94.72.104.81 (24GB RAM, 8 cores); parallel Vercel config maintained
meta.method: Generated from 6 parallel codebase exploration passes (frontend routes, API routes, services/integrations, components/state, database migrations, infrastructure). Counts derived from file enumeration; descriptions verified by reading representative source files.

stack.ui: React ^19.2.6, Tailwind CSS ^3.4.11, shadcn/ui on Radix primitives, framer-motion
stack.clientState: React Context + useReducer (feature-scoped) + localStorage persistence; zustand ^4.4.7 for 2 lib-level stores only
stack.icons: Phosphor via react-icons/pi (~452 files); @iconify/react declared but unused
stack.ai: @google/genai (CMS content/image gen, CPQ AI), internal Claude agent toolkit (lib/agents)

routeGroups.name: Admin Portal
routeGroups.description: Internal staff back-office: products, coverage, network/NOC, billing, CPQ/quotes, orders, CRM, integrations, sales engine. Largest surface of the app.

routeGroups.name: Customer Dashboard
routeGroups.description: Authenticated consumer self-service: services, invoices, statements, payment methods, usage, KYC, tickets.

routeGroups.name: Order / Checkout Funnel
routeGroups.description: Multi-step consumer ordering: coverage, package, address, contact, OTP verification, payment, confirmation. State via OrderContext + localStorage.

routeGroups.name: Partner Portal
routeGroups.description: Reseller workspace: leads, feasibility checks, commissions, onboarding, marketing resources. Client-side auth (not middleware-gated).

routeGroups.name: Auth Flows
routeGroups.description: Login, register, OTP verify, password reset, OAuth callback.

routeGroups.name: B2B Customer Portal
routeGroups.description: Business-customer portal: multi-site management, billing, support. Middleware-gated.

routeGroups.name: Ambassador Portal
routeGroups.description: Referral-ambassador program: earnings, referral codes, marketing assets. Middleware-gated.

routeGroups.name: Sales Agents Portal
routeGroups.description: Field/sales agent workspace with dashboard and login (client-side auth).

routeGroups.name: Marketing Route Group
routeGroups.description: Parenthesized route group: top-level campaign/segment landing pages.

routeGroups.name: Product & Service Marketing
routeGroups.description: Public product catalogue and offer landing pages: products, connectivity, cloud, wireless/SkyFibre, bundles, deals, pricing.

routeGroups.name: Content & Informational
routeGroups.description: Blog, resources, locations, coverage checkers, status, FAQ, contact, dynamic root [slug] pages.

routeGroups.name: Commerce, Payments & Quotes
routeGroups.description: Standalone checkout, invoice pay links, quote sharing, order/package lookup outside the /order funnel.

routeGroups.name: Onboarding & Utility
routeGroups.description: Tokenized/utility flows: onboarding, WhatsApp opt-in, unsubscribe, Unjani forms, technician field app, WorkConnect.

rootLayout.notes: Inter/Manrope/JetBrains Mono fonts, GTM + gtag, WhatsAppFloatingButton, StickyMobileCTA, export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'.

notes: Middleware gates /admin, /portal, /ambassadors; /partner, /dashboard, /agents rely on client-side auth (CustomerAuthProvider / useAdminAuth).
Admin auth has a dev+localhost bypass injecting a mock admin user (no login in local dev).
Consumer dashboard exists at both app/dashboard/ and app/(dashboard)/dashboard/ — potential route collision to verify.
Legacy/duplicate marketing surfaces: /cloud vs /cloud-hosting, /products vs /product, /coverage vs /coverage-check.

groups.name: Admin / Back-office
groups.description: Dominant API surface (~55%): billing (32), orders (18), network/MikroTik (15), integrations (26), customers, products, CRM, analytics. Nearly all routes call authenticateAdmin (lib/auth/admin-api-auth).

groups.name: Sales Engine
groups.description: Geospatial B2B sales targeting: zone discovery, demographics/POI import, coverage analysis, pipeline health, competitor intel, Slack briefings.

groups.name: Competitor Analysis
groups.description: Scrapes and compares competitor ISP products/pricing.

groups.name: Coverage
groups.description: Coverage/feasibility checks with lead capture; MTN WMS tile proxy; SkyFibre orderability; packages by location.

groups.name: Scheduled Cron Jobs
groups.description: CRON_SECRET-authenticated scheduled tasks: invoice generation, debit orders, payment reconciliation, Zoho sync, AR/stats snapshots, competitor scrape, supplier sync.

groups.name: B2B Quotes
groups.description: Business quote lifecycle: create, PDF, email/share, e-signature, approval workflow, token-shared public views.

groups.name: Customer Dashboard API
groups.description: Customer self-service: services, orders, invoices (pay/PDF), payment methods, usage, statements, KYC sessions.

groups.name: Partner Portal API
groups.description: Partner leads, feasibility (with AI chat), commissions, compliance docs, marketing assets.

groups.name: Ruijie Networking
groups.description: Ruijie Cloud device management: inventory, metrics, clients, reboot, tunnels — cached in Supabase, synced via Inngest.

groups.name: Payments v2 (provider-abstracted)
groups.description: Provider-factory payment layer (NetCash primary): initiate, tokenize, status, redirect, webhook (auto-creates order + triggers RICA).

groups.name: Payment (legacy NetCash)
groups.description: Older single-provider NetCash routes: initiate, tokenize, e-mandate, webhook. Parallel to /api/payments.

groups.name: CPQ Engines
groups.description: Two CPQ stacks: generic AI-assisted CPQ (analyze/recommend, eligibility, pricing rules, sessions) and MITS-specific CPQ (modules, tiers, pricing calc).

groups.name: Consumer Orders
groups.description: Order creation/lifecycle: create-pending, mobile deals, terms acceptance, device registration, drafts.

groups.name: Compliance / KYC
groups.description: KYC/KYB workflow tied to quotes: sessions, uploads, approve/decline, Didit webhook (HMAC-verified).

groups.name: Authentication API
groups.description: Phone signup, OTP send/verify/login, email verification, password reset.

groups.name: Public Product Catalogue
groups.description: Product listings, add-ons, MTN deals + recommendations, TMF-style productOffering, hardware terms, promotions.

groups.name: B2B Portal API
groups.description: Portal-user surface: billing, multi-site health, support.

groups.name: Contracts / e-Sign
groups.description: Contract generation from quotes, PDF download, send-for-signature, Zoho Sign webhook (HMAC).

groups.name: Notifications & Messaging
groups.description: Notification feed/preferences, order confirmations, WhatsApp opt-in, announcements, unsubscribe.

groups.name: Inbound Webhook Hub
groups.description: Receivers for Clickatell SMS delivery, NetCash e-mandate/billing, Resend email events, WhatsApp, Interstellio diagnostics.

groups.name: Zoho Integration API
groups.description: Zoho OAuth/sync/webhook plus MCP passthrough client (Books/Desk/Sign).

groups.name: B2B Onboarding & Services
groups.description: B2B journey/site details, customer ensure, PPPoE credential reveal, onboarding submission.

groups.name: Public Pay Links
groups.description: Public invoice payment links; invoice creation from contracts.

groups.name: Lead Capture
groups.description: Public lead intake: licensed-wireless, no-coverage, generic.

groups.name: Inngest Handler
groups.description: Single serve() endpoint registering ~30 background functions.

groups.name: Support / Field / Sales Actors
groups.description: Support tickets, technician jobs, sales agents, ambassador codes, MTN deals.

groups.name: Utility / Misc
groups.description: Geocode, health/status, tracking, diagnostics, preview, uploads, KYC upload, service activation + RICA, test routes.

authPatterns.detail: ~429 routes use createClientWithSession() + supabase.auth.getUser() for customer/portal auth. APIs check BOTH Authorization Bearer header AND cookies (ref commit ac642e8).
Admin routes call authenticateAdmin from lib/auth/admin-api-auth; JWT admin claim fast path (15-min API TTL) with admin_users table fallback.
~107 routes use the service-role client (bypasses RLS): webhooks, cron, background/system operations.
/api/cron/* verify a CRON_SECRET Bearer token (~21 of 23 enforce it).
Didit (X-Didit-Signature) and Zoho Sign (X-Zoho-Sign-Signature) use HMAC-SHA256 verification; WhatsApp uses the Meta GET verification handshake.

webhooks.notes: Auto-creates order + triggers RICA on payment.completed

stateManagement.primary: React Context + useReducer/useState per feature, localStorage persistence, Supabase hydration; React Query for server state; Zustand only for lib/auth/admin-auth-store.ts and lib/cms/store.ts.

designSystem.typography: Inter (sans/body/data), Manrope (heading/display), JetBrains Mono; 1.32x modular scale
designSystem.icons: Phosphor via react-icons/pi (primary, ~452 files); @iconify/react declared but zero imports; custom RandIcon

modules.name: Inngest Background Jobs

database.criticalRule: Billing uses customer_invoices, NOT invoices (a legacy public.invoices table exists in the baseline but is inactive).

patterns.rls: ~260 ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + ~485 CREATE POLICY statements — RLS is the default posture.
patterns.functions: ~190 PL/pgSQL functions incl. reference-number generators and geospatial helpers.
patterns.triggers: ~162 triggers: updated_at maintenance, audit logging, auto-numbering.
patterns.postgis: PostGIS geography SRID 4326 for coverage + proximity (ST_DWithin, ST_Distance); dedicated spatial-query optimization migration 2026-07-14.
patterns.structure: 20260523000000_baseline_squash.sql holds the bulk of the schema; 24 incremental migrations follow.

steps.name: Supabase client
steps.description: @supabase/ssr edge client with request/response cookie sync.

steps.name: Admin auth
steps.description: Gates /admin/*. Dev+localhost mock-admin bypass. JWT admin-claim fast path (1h TTL) with admin_users fallback; workspace access via canAccessAdminPath(role, path, tenant.modules).

steps.name: Ambassador auth
steps.description: Gates /ambassadors/*; status verification deferred to client to avoid redirect loops.

steps.name: Request ID + response
steps.description: Sets x-request-id (crypto.randomUUID) and returns response with cookie updates.

auth.model: Three-context auth: Consumer, Partner (B2B), Admin — plus Ambassador portal. Providers exclude /admin and /partners paths to prevent context mixing.

supabaseClients.name: createClient (server, service-role)
supabaseClients.detail: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY; bypasses RLS; getUser() returns null. For elevated ops, cron, webhooks.

supabaseClients.name: createClientWithSession (server, cookie)
supabaseClients.detail: Anon key + next/headers cookies; respects RLS; required for auth.getUser().

supabaseClients.name: createClient (browser)
supabaseClients.detail: Anon key singleton, localStorage session, RLS enforced.

supabaseClients.name: createMiddlewareSupabaseClient (edge)
supabaseClients.detail: Edge cookie-sync client for middleware session reads.

auth.keyRule: APIs check BOTH Authorization Bearer header AND session cookies to avoid 401s (commit ac642e8).

workflows.name: Production Deploy
workflows.detail: Memory freeing, disk guards, tar node_modules cache, rsync SWC cache, Turbopack build (heap 12288MB), docker build --no-cache, commit-SHA verification, compose up, health loop.

workflows.name: Staging Deploy
workflows.detail: Same pipeline; circletel-staging container on Traefik, port 3001, staging.circletel.co.za. Turbopack validated here first.

workflows.name: PR -> Staging (opt-in)
workflows.detail: Force-pushes PR HEAD to staging branch.

workflows.name: PR Quality Checks
workflows.detail: type-check/lint/build all continue-on-error (repo carries ~295 pre-existing TS errors); blocking gates: Dockerfile validation (heap >= 8192, output standalone), brand-literal ratchet, type-error ratchet.

cicd.prePushHook: .githooks/pre-push (via core.hooksPath): build-config check (heap >= 6144, cpus env-driven) + scoped type-check that only blocks on errors in files you touched.

deployment.strategy: 2-branch: feature -> staging -> main. Staging validates (incl. Turbopack) before production.

production.image: Built natively on VPS runner (same machine as prod host — no registry pull); Dockerfile is a thin node:20-alpine runner copying .next/standalone; Chromium installed for Puppeteer; HEALTHCHECK /api/health with 120s start (254-page cold start).

deployment.vercel: Parallel vercel.json target: heap 12288, per-route function limits, ~23 cron schedules. next.config.js branches on VERCEL env.
deployment.buildNotes: output: standalone; experimental.cpus env-driven (BUILD_CPUS, default 1); optimizePackageImports for react-icons/date-fns/recharts/zod/framer-motion/react-query; eslint+ts errors ignored during builds.

commands.typeCheck: npm run type-check:memory (4GB heap) — mandatory before commit
commands.scripts: set -a && source .env.local && set +a && npx tsx scripts/x.ts (dotenv/config does NOT load .env.local)

testing.unit: Jest 30 via next/jest; coverage gate scoped to lib/payments, lib/types, app/api/payments (85-95% thresholds).
testing.e2e: Playwright: desktop (localhost:3005), mobile (iPhone 13/Pixel 5/iPad Mini), staging, manual configs.
testing.ci: Path-filtered payment + API integration workflows; pr-checks type/lint/build non-blocking.

findings.finding: Admin surface dominates: 335 of 608 endpoints (~55%) and ~238 of ~700 component files.
Two parallel payment stacks: /api/payment + lib/payment (legacy NetCash-only) vs /api/payments + lib/payments (provider-abstracted). Both define a netcash-service.ts — in-progress consolidation.
Two OrderContext implementations coexist: components/order/context/OrderContext.tsx (canonical, useReducer) and contexts/OrderContext.tsx (useState, likely legacy).
StatusBadge/StatCard/SectionCard trio duplicated in components/admin/shared, components/shared, and components/backend.
Consumer dashboard exists at both app/dashboard/ and app/(dashboard)/dashboard/ — verify which resolves /dashboard.
Two product-catalogue models coexist: operational products/service_packages vs admin_products + approval queue feeding offers/CPQ. Also legacy public.invoices vs canonical customer_invoices.
Two CPQ stacks by design: generic AI CPQ (/api/cpq) and MITS CPQ (/api/mits-cpq).
@iconify/react declared in package.json with zero import sites; hooks/use-mobile duplicated as .ts and .tsx.
Admin middleware has a dev+localhost bypass (mock admin user). /partner, /dashboard, /agents are client-side-auth only (not middleware-gated).
Type-check/lint/build never block PRs (repo carries ~295 pre-existing TS errors); only Dockerfile validation and the brand-literal/type-error ratchets can fail a PR.
No Sentry integration; error handling via lib/errors + lib/logging with x-request-id correlation.
